D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Radiation Pressure

shine a beam on a sail and watch its momentum push the sail forward

Each photon carries momentum p = E/c; when it strikes a surface, it transfers that momentum — light exerts a measurable pressure.

Slide the intensity from candle-faint to solar-bright. Toggle absorber/reflector: a perfect reflector receives twice the pressure because each photon reverses its momentum.
